question: Using the 2017-2022 program fiscal year with the highest federal share obligated for Hazard Mitigation Assistance projects, form the school-year window from two years before through two years after. For Texas counties that are top five by average summer meal program sites in that window, what is the highest average site count, rounded to the nearest integer? Return the rounded number in `Number` format. Your response should be in the format [Answer].

1.fact: Summing federalShareObligated by programFy for 2017-2022, the maximum total is 48932227.55 in 2020.
1.subquestion: Which program fiscal year from 2017-2022 has the highest total federal share obligated for funded projects under the Hazard Mitigation Assistance grant programs? (Sum federalShareObligated by programFy and select the maximum.)

2.fact: In 2018-2019 (<node_1 answer> - 2 school year), the Texas counties with the most summer meal program sites were: HARRIS (1052), DALLAS (1014), HIDALGO (494), BEXAR (411), TARRANT (367).
2.subquestion: Which Texas counties had the most summer meal program sites in <node_1 answer> - 2 school year? (Filter: ProgramYear == '<node_1 answer - 2>-<node_1 answer - 1 (two digits)>'; count sites by SiteCounty; take top 5.)

3.fact: In 2019-2020 (<node_1 answer> - 1 school year), the Texas counties with the most summer meal program sites were: HARRIS (1919), DALLAS (1479), BEXAR (1038), TARRANT (688), HIDALGO (515).
3.subquestion: Which Texas counties had the most summer meal program sites in <node_1 answer> - 1 school year? (Filter: ProgramYear == '<node_1 answer - 1>-<node_1 answer (two digits)>'; count sites by SiteCounty; take top 5.)

4.fact: In 2020-2021 (<node_1 answer> school year), the Texas counties with the most summer meal program sites were: HARRIS (2674), DALLAS (1504), BEXAR (1168), TARRANT (1033), HIDALGO (700).
4.subquestion: Which Texas counties had the most summer meal program sites in <node_1 answer> school year? (Filter: ProgramYear == '<node_1 answer>-<node_1 answer + 1 (two digits)>'; count sites by SiteCounty; take top 5.)

5.fact: In 2021-2022 (<node_1 answer> + 1 school year), the Texas counties with the most summer meal program sites were: HARRIS (1825), DALLAS (1545), BEXAR (814), TARRANT (757), HIDALGO (526).
5.subquestion: Which Texas counties had the most summer meal program sites in <node_1 answer> + 1 school year? (Filter: ProgramYear == '<node_1 answer + 1>-<node_1 answer + 2 (two digits)>'; count sites by SiteCounty; take top 5.)

6.fact: In 2022-2023 (<node_1 answer> + 2 school year), the Texas counties with the most summer meal program sites were: HARRIS (1016), DALLAS (688), BEXAR (364), HIDALGO (353), TARRANT (303).
6.subquestion: Which Texas counties had the most summer meal program sites in <node_1 answer> + 2 school year? (Filter: ProgramYear == '<node_1 answer + 2>-<node_1 answer + 3 (two digits)>'; count sites by SiteCounty; take top 5.)

reasoning_chain: Node 1: Program fiscal year with highest hazard mitigation federal share obligated (2017-2022) -> 2020
Derived school year range: 2018-2019 through 2022-2023 (node_1 answer - 2 to node_1 answer + 2 school years)
AGGREGATION: Compute average across all 5 years -> Top 5 are {HARRIS: 1697.2, DALLAS: 1246.0, BEXAR: 759.0, TARRANT: 629.6, HIDALGO: 517.6}
Result: Highest average is Harris at 1697.2 -> rounded to 1697
